title: Government of Canada Delivers on Climate Competitiveness Strategy Commitment to Lower Methane Emissions from Major Sources
content: The Honourable Julie Dabrusin, Minister of the Environment, Climate Change and Nature, announced final regulations to reduce methane emissions from the oil and gas sector and landfills across Canada, marking a major step forward in the Climate Competitiveness Strategy. The Enhanced Methane Regulations apply to onshore production and LNG facilities, strengthening leak detection and venting controls. These measures are expected to realize a cumulative greenhouse gas emissions reduction of 304 megatonnes of carbon dioxide equivalent by 2040 while supporting over 130 methane abatement companies. Additionally, new Landfill Methane Regulations require owners to monitor surfaces and repair leaks, aiming to reduce emissions by 100 megatonnes. To further support these goals, Minister Dabrusin announced nearly $16 million in funding through the Energy Innovation Program for eight projects focused on methane measurement and mitigation. These investments aim to accelerate the clean tech industry and solidify Canadaâ€™s commitment to addressing climate change.
author: Analysts Account
